In a gambit to learn more about their mysterious pursuers, the king and his mesmer escort, tired and tattered, had set a trap for a mursaat who had wandered further ahead of their hunting party. On their own, a single mursaat was formidable—deadly, but not invincible. Even with their foe defeated, one of Gauvain's students fell in the skirmish and King Jadon sustained serious injuries attempting to protect the young spell-slinger. The rest attempted to carry the king afterward, but he would succumb to his wounds the following day.

In a gambit to learn more about their mysterious pursuers, the king and his mesmer escort, tired and tattered, had set a trap for a mursaat who had wandered further ahead of their hunting party. On their own, a single mursaat was formidable—deadly, but not invincible. Even with their foe defeated, one of Gauvain's students fell in the skirmish and King Jadon sustained serious injuries attempting to protect the young spell-slinger. The rest attempted to carry the king afterward, but he would succumb to his wounds the following day.    The remaining four continued into South Kessex, toward what King Jadon thought could be a possible safe haven. During their detour to "bury" the dead, the mursaat had made up for lost ground and would eventually surround them. The duchess and her comrades stared down their attackers as the Veil of Ilya revealed their presence once more. In a blinding moment of flashing sigils and spellcraft, the four were dazed by the light—only to find the mursaat lying dead around them.    Before them appeared the projection of a man who introduced himself as Obryn—a seemingly powerful spellcaster in his own right. Their mysterious savior would help them find proper shelter near the village of Shaemoor and keep them informed as best he could.

In recognition of your exemplary performance to date, your legion has promoted you to centurion. Congratulations, soldier.But don't celebrate too hard. This isn't a ceremonial promotion: higher rank means higher expectations, more responsibility, and more important duties.Starting now: I have your first assignment waiting. Report to me in my office as soon as possible for your orders.On a more personal note, let me say how glad I am to have another fighting soldier among the brass. Welcome aboard.—Rytlock Brimstone, Blood Legion Tribune

In the depths of Maguuma, we scaled the twisted spire inside the massive tree guarding the jungle's heart. With the Jungle Dragon distracted by the full-scale assault happening outside, my allies and I infiltrated the structure and found Marshal Trahearne. After discussing the situation, Trahearne informed us that, because he was now part of the Jungle Dragon, he could serve as a conduit into Modremoth's mind. We formulated a plan to challenge him in a final confrontation.
In the distant hinterlands, life struggles upward deep in a hidden cavern.
In the Dream, you saw a huntsman wearing armor the color of emerald leaves. What could it mean? Perhaps, one day, you will meet him.

In the dry streambed where there was once only dust, water is flowing again. And for residents of the small village of Redplain, hope is flowing with it, after years of hardship following the diversion of the Ullen River. While appeals to the local authority have still gone unanswered, help has arrived in an unusual form.The benefactor, a hydrological engineer from Rata Sum who didn't give residents a name, has taken it upon himself to reroute a branch of the river through the village before reconnecting with the main flow, allowing vital access without disrupting the waterway's eventual destination.But the reactions have not all been positive. Administrator Grozz, who oversees the river, was furious in his response: "We cannot have miscreants interfering with our work without filing a report, having a formal survey done, paying the necessary fees, and allowing for our direct oversight. There is protocol and procedure to these things. If he were to go through the proper channels, an assessment could be started as soon as six months from now."As for the engineer in question, he left as soon as the work was done, and we have not been able to reach him for comment.

In the far north, just beyond civilization, there exists an expanse of tundra overrun by Svanir and Jormag's icebrood. It is believed that Jormag has taken up residence nearby. And that the Elder Dragon's influence corrupts all who go there. The only safe place is a kodan sanctuary where the quaggan and kodan residents stubbornly refuse to give up the fight.

In the library of the Guild Initiative Headquarters you will find guild hall explorers. Each explorer has identified a different location your guild can liberate and claim as your guild hall. When you've chosen the one you want, have one of your guild leaders talk to that explorer to fund an expedition to begin the process of claiming the hall.

In the murk, the Bog Queen stirs.  Her hands and teeth nip at your fur.  Don't let her voice into your thoughts,   Or else her screams shall make them rot.     So plug your ears and clean your paws,  Don't let her pick apart your flaws.  You draw your spear, she'll hide beneath.  Steady now—she'll sense your heat.     So long as dead shall fail to rise,  That wicked queen bears no surprise.  Listen close, don't say a word.  Valravn alone know what you heard.     Now close your eyes, and she'll be gone,  Back to the bog—just wait for dawn.

In the weeks following, after the charr invasion of Kryta was routed by the White Mantle and their masters, their occupation of Kryta began. The remaining mesmers decided they would not abandon Kryta to its new regime. There, Adelaide, Velise, Gauvain, Nemah, and Obryn would form the first inner council of the Mesmer Collective, a fledging network of spies and informants that would serve Kryta in the formative years of the Shining Blade and, later, their own kinsfolk in the Ascalonian settlement. In time, the Collective's numbers would grow. Individuals with ties and connections to larger Krytan towns would form the first outer council and, eventually, the first members of the Shroud.

In what is more colloquially known as the "Pantless Incident of '04," norn hunter Sven Liefson risked greatly damaging norn/human relations with irresponsible consumption of drink. After several rounds, a visibly intoxicated Liefson challenged a nearby human to a test of strength. When the human settler repeatedly declined his challenge, Liefson mocked him, going as far to remove his pants and shake his behind in the human's face. This incident serves as an excellent teaching moment for key faux pas to avoid in interspecies communications. After sobering up a few days later, Liefson apologized to the human in question. The two are said to have become great friends following the debacle.

Incident Number 329Incident Report Filed by: Captain Fa, Xunlai GuardOn the morning of Songtahn 21st, 0330 hours, 1843, Xunlai Security responded to a silent alarm emanating from the lobby of the Yong reactor complex. Xunlai Security mechs were activated, but the signal was lost shortly after.In response, Xunlai Security personnel were dispatched to the facility from the surface. Per standard procedure, two units were dispatched: one general security team, and one squad of elite units who have knowledge of the "asset." Upon arrival in the lobby, the remains of six jade tech guard mechs were discovered strewn across the entryway.While general security was ordered to secure the lobby, the elite guard proceeded down to the reactor core. In the reactor room, the elite guard encountered a single perpetrator attempting to sabotage a nearby security console. When confronted, the perpetrator, whom Xunlai Security has now been able to identify as one Mai Trin, initially made gestures of hostile intent but upon seeing that she was outnumbered, wisely opted to stand down.The perpetrator is being held in a maximum security Xunlai Jade facility off-site. We've received orders directly from Lady Joon that she wishes to speak privately with the perpetrator. Please see attached addendum.

Incident Report A-0119Internal Security DirectorateCOMPLAINT: Krewe chief reports disappearance of researcher (Subject A).INVESTIGATION: Masticated and digested remains of Subject A discovered in specimen enclosure. Tranquilizer dart discovered among remains. Suggests unnatural death.Interview with fellow researcher (Subject B) indicates motive and results in admission of guilt. Subject A apparently took credit for Subject B's work and had the gall to smirk at him while doing so.FINDINGS: Subject B's actions, while not recommended, were found to be justifiable.
